newPage.title: New page
newPage.conflict: A page with this title already exists

ingest.title: Ingest Activity
ingest.empty: No ingest runs yet. Click "Run Ingest" to start.
ingest.emptyBody: Upload a document or trigger an ingest run to let Synapse extract pages, links, review items, and graph relationships.
ingest.noRunSelected: Select a run to see details.
ingest.zeroPagesHint: 0 pages created — check the provider configuration and that the file format is supported.

upload.label: Upload document
upload.drop: Drop a file here, or click to browse

settings.title: Settings

general.desc: Tune how much context Synapse can carry into model calls and how that budget is split.
general.note: Use this when answers feel too short, too forgetful, or too expensive.
general.contextWindowExplainer: Larger windows preserve more history and retrieved wiki context, but can increase latency and cost.
general.budgetExplainer: The split below is applied automatically so chat, retrieval, system instructions, and generation each get predictable room.

settings.contextWindowHelp: Maximum tokens sent to the model per request.

nav.groupEssentialsDesc: The minimum to get Synapse working.
nav.groupContentDesc: Where your data comes from and how the wiki is written.
nav.groupAiBehaviorDesc: How the AI retrieves and reasons. Advanced — the defaults are fine to start.
nav.groupAccessDesc: Who can reach Synapse and how to connect other tools.

llmModels.title: LLM Models
llmModels.desc: One row per provider. Changes take effect immediately. API keys are stored as environment variables — not in this UI.
llmModels.note: Providers drive chat, ingest synthesis, review assistance, and deep research. Vault-scoped rows override global rows for this vault.
llmModels.addProviderHelp: Add the model identity and optional base URL. Secrets stay outside the UI; configure them in the runtime environment.
llmModels.lastProviderWarning: This is the last configured provider. Chat and ingest will fail until a new provider is added.
llmModels.noProvidersHelp: Add at least one provider before using chat, ingest generation, or deep research.
llmModels.vaultProviderHelp: Only this vault will prefer this provider.
llmModels.globalProviderHelp: All vaults can use this provider unless a vault-specific row overrides it.
llmModels.introGuide: Choose how Synapse thinks: API (recommended), Local (Ollama, offline), or CLI (your Claude subscription). If you pick CLI, paste its token in the CLI authentication box below.
llmModels.catalogDesc: One row per vendor. Toggle to enable. Only one vendor is active at a time — changes save instantly.
llmModels.apiKeyNoStorage: Key storage requires SYNAPSE_SECRET_KEY to be set in the backend environment.
llmModels.codexAuthNote: Codex CLI authenticates itself — run `codex login` on the host (OAuth) or set OPENAI_API_KEY. Synapse stores no Codex token (unlike the Claude subscription token).

embeddings.title: Vector Embeddings
embeddings.desc: Enable semantic search against your wiki. Works with OpenAI-compatible /v1/embeddings endpoints, Ollama native embeddings, and other local models. No API key needed for local models.
embeddings.note: Embeddings decide whether retrieval understands meaning or only exact words. This page is read-only because the backend owns the runtime setting.
embeddings.envNote: Read-only — configured via environment variables (EMBEDDING_URL, EMBEDDING_MODEL, EMBEDDING_DIM). Edit your .env or docker-compose.yml to change.
embeddings.error: Could not load embedding config. Is the backend running?
embeddings.lexicalOnlyNote: Retrieval is running keyword-only (title + body match). Controlled by the EMBEDDINGS_ENABLED environment variable. URL, model, and dimensions below are informational only in this mode.
embeddings.semanticModeHelp: Chat and wiki search can match related concepts even when the exact words differ.
embeddings.lexicalModeHelp: Results depend more on titles and exact wording. This is safer for lightweight installs, but less flexible for discovery.

sourceWatch.desc: Watch a source folder and import changed files on a predictable schedule.
sourceWatch.note: Use this for ongoing notes, exported docs, or folders that should keep the wiki fresh without manual uploads.
sourceWatch.howItWorks: Synapse scans the configured directory, queues supported files, and records each run in Sources so you can inspect cost and errors.

apiMcp.title: API + MCP
apiMcp.desc: Expose Synapse to your own tools through the local HTTP API, and optionally through the bundled MCP server for agent clients.
apiMcp.note: Use this when Codex, Claude Desktop, claude.ai, or another agent should read from or write to your wiki.
apiMcp.error: Could not load MCP server info. Is the backend running?

access.title: Access
access.why: Remote MCP can expose wiki tools outside the app. Keep token access on unless you are testing from a trusted private network.
access.revealWarning: Copy now — this token will not be shown again.
access.allowWithoutTokenCaveat: Token-less access applies only to private sources (loopback, LAN, Tailscale). Public access via Cloudflare tunnel always requires a token — this is enforced server-side and cannot be bypassed.

remote.title: Remote (claude.ai)
remote.when: Enable remote MCP only when an external client needs to connect over HTTP. Local Claude Desktop can use the stdio config below.
remote.noTokenNote: Generate a token or enable token-less access above to enable remote access.

output.title: Output Preferences
output.desc: Controls for generated content — language, conversation history length.
output.note: These settings affect how Synapse speaks and how much prior conversation it carries forward.
output.languageHelp: Language changes labels and model-facing output preferences where the app supports them.
output.convHistoryDesc: Number of prior messages sent to the AI on each request. More = richer context but more tokens.
output.memoryTradeoff: More messages help follow long threads; fewer messages reduce token use and keep answers focused.

interface.title: Interface
interface.note: The current interface is optimized for dense wiki work: scanning, comparing, reviewing, and jumping between sections.
interface.currentHelp: Navigation labels, compact cards, and responsive stacking are active. Theme and density controls will become editable later.
interface.comingSoon: Theme and density options — coming soon.

theme.label: Theme
theme.help: Choose how the app appearance adapts to your environment.

maintenance.title: Maintenance
maintenance.desc: Tools for cleaning up the wiki — detect and merge duplicate entities/concepts that the LLM created under different names across re-ingests.
maintenance.note: Use this area carefully: maintenance actions affect how pages and concepts remain connected over time.
maintenance.duplicatesDesc: Asks the LLM to scan all entity / concept pages and group ones that likely refer to the same topic under different names (English vs Italian, plural vs singular, abbreviation vs full form). You confirm each group before merging.
maintenance.resetDesc: Clears all locally stored settings and panel layout.
maintenance.resetImpact: This resets local UI preferences only. It does not delete wiki pages, sources, providers, or MCP server data.

about.title: About
about.desc: Synapse is a self-hosted LLM Wiki workspace for turning sources, chat, and research into linked knowledge.
about.note: The product direction is LLM Wiki plus an agent-ready local workbench: readable by humans, useful to tools.
about.positioning: Compared with a plain note importer, Synapse is designed around review loops, graph exploration, provider choice, and MCP access.

changelog.title: Changelog
changelog.desc: Release history and notable changes (Keep a Changelog format).
changelog.unavailable: Changelog unavailable. Check the build output.
changelog.emptyBody: No content recorded for this version.
changelog.footer: Showing the 10 most recent releases — full history in

webClipper.desc: Configure the Chrome MV3 web clipper extension (F11). Clips are sent to this Synapse instance and auto-ingested via the watcher.
webClipper.error: Could not load web clipper config. Is the backend running?
webClipper.enabledHelp: When disabled, POST /clip returns 503 and the extension cannot ingest.
webClipper.revealWarning: Copy now — this token will not be shown again.
webClipper.originsHelp: Comma-separated extra origins (e.g. https://example.com). Loopback origins are always allowed.
webClipper.extensionHint: Load the extension from the extension/ folder in this repository (Chrome → Manage Extensions → Load unpacked).

webSearch.desc: SearXNG is the only supported web-search backend (I9). Used by Deep Research to fetch sources. Configure the URL and categories here.
webSearch.error: Could not load web search config. Is the backend running?
webSearch.urlHelp: Must be a valid http:// or https:// URL. The URL is not secret — it is displayed here in full.
webSearch.urlValidationError: Must be a valid http:// or https:// URL.
webSearch.categoriesHelp: Comma-separated SearXNG categories. Leave empty to use SearXNG defaults.
webSearch.maxQueriesHelp: Maximum SearXNG queries per deep-research iteration (1–50).
webSearch.clearHelp: Falls back to SEARXNG_URL env var and code defaults.
webSearch.searxngOnly: Only SearXNG is supported as the web-search backend. No other provider can be configured here (I9).

cliAuth.title: CLI Subscription Auth
cliAuth.error: Could not load CLI auth config. Is the backend running?
cliAuth.tokenHelp: Paste the token printed by `claude setup-token` on the host (starts with sk-ant-oat01-).
cliAuth.saveError: Failed to save. Is the backend running?
cliAuth.guideSteps:
1) On the host run: claude setup-token
2) Paste the printed sk-ant-oat01-… token here
3) Leave ANTHROPIC_API_KEY unset — the DB token is injected into the Claude CLI and the API key is scrubbed from its environment, so your subscription is used ($0 per token).

scenarios.title: Scenario Templates
scenarios.desc: Apply a predefined scenario to quickly configure your vault with a purpose, schema, and structure template.
scenarios.applyConfirmBody: Applying "{{name}}" will overwrite your vault's purpose.md and schema.md. Existing wiki pages are not affected.
scenarios.loadError: Could not load scenarios. Is the backend running?

security.title: Security
security.desc: Manage the bearer token used to authenticate this client against the Synapse backend.
security.tokenPresent: Token configured — requests carry the Authorization header.
security.tokenAbsent: No token stored. Leave blank if authentication is disabled on the server.
security.asymmetryNote: To rotate the server token: set SYNAPSE_AUTH_TOKEN to a new value in your docker-compose.yml and restart the container. Then paste the new token here. This UI only updates the client copy — no server call is made.
security.cfDesc: For non-browser clients (desktop app, iOS, clipper) when the backend sits behind Cloudflare Access. Paste the service-token Client ID and Secret so requests pass the edge gate. Leave blank in the browser — the interactive-login cookie handles it there.
security.cfPresent: Service token configured — requests carry the CF-Access headers.
security.cfAbsent: No service token stored. Needed only when the backend is behind Cloudflare Access.

opsSchedule.title: Job scheduling
opsSchedule.sectionDesc: Control when lint and domain-classification jobs run automatically.
opsSchedule.desc: Configure automatic schedule and trigger manual runs for background jobs.
opsSchedule.fetchError: Could not load schedule status. Is the backend running?
opsSchedule.dormantHint: This job is dormant — configure the domain vocabulary first (Settings › Advanced › Runtime overrides › Domain vocabulary).

opDesc.lint: Lint scan — finds structural and semantic problems; fixes remain manual.
opDesc.backfill: Domain classification — tags new or unclassified pages against the vocabulary.
opDesc.schema_review: Schema review — proposes schema.md updates in the Review queue based on how the vault actually evolved.
opDesc.reclassify: Re-assigns page types per the curated schema.md rules — new/untyped/concept pages by default.

costs.title: Cost & Usage
costs.desc: Monthly AI spend aggregated from all runs. Refreshes manually — no background polling.
costs.error: Could not load cost data. Is the backend running?
costs.noData: No cost data for this period.

chat.title: Chat
chat.noConversations: No conversations yet. Click + to start.
chat.empty: Send a message to start chatting.
chat.emptyTitle: Ask your wiki, not just a model
chat.emptyLead: Synapse can answer with retrieved context, turn useful replies into wiki pages, and help you find the next connection in the graph.
chat.emptyPromptMap: Map the strongest concepts in this vault
chat.emptyPromptSummarize: Summarize what changed in the latest sources
chat.emptyPromptResearch: Find gaps that need deep research
chat.inputPlaceholder: Ask anything… (Enter to send, Shift+Enter for newline)
chat.inputHint: Enter to send · Shift+Enter for newline

examples.q1: Map the strongest concepts in this vault
examples.q2: Summarize what changed in the latest sources
examples.q3: Find gaps that need deep research

chat.citationNotFound: Cited page not found in the wiki
chat.attachImageDisabled: The active provider does not support images
chat.webSearchOn: Web search on — click to disable
chat.webSearchOff: Web search off — click to enable

research.title: Deep Search
research.topicHint: Synapse will search the web, assess sources, and synthesize a wiki page.
research.empty: No research runs yet. Enter a topic and click Start Research.
research.emptyBody: Start with a focused question; Synapse will collect sources, track cost, and save useful synthesis back into the wiki.
research.providerMissing: Configure an LLM provider before starting deep research.
research.exampleTwo: Find gaps in the latest imported sources
research.noRunSelected: Select a run to see its detail and synthesis.

topicDialog.title: Start Deep Research
topicDialog.optimizeUnavailable: Topic optimization unavailable — edit the topic and queries manually.
topicDialog.noQueries: No queries yet — click Add query or the LLM will generate them at runtime.

review.title: Review Queue
review.hint: AI proposals — create a page, start deep research, or skip.
review.empty: No proposals. Ingest some documents to populate the review queue.
review.emptyBody: After ingest, the LLM proposes follow-up work: missing pages, research gaps, contradictions, duplicates, and confirmations. You decide what to act on.
review.createFailed: Page generation failed — retry or skip.
review.searxngUnavailable: Deep research is unavailable: SEARXNG_URL is not configured on the backend.
review.sweepHelp: Run the auto-resolution sweep: closes missing-page and duplicate proposals whose page now exists, and optionally asks the LLM to close stale suggestions.
review.sweepResult: Sweep complete: {{rule}} rule-resolved, {{llm}} LLM-resolved, {{kept}} kept pending.
review.bulkResult: Bulk action complete: {{updated}} updated, {{skipped}} already resolved.
review.clearResolvedHelp: Hard-delete all terminal rows (skipped, dismissed, created, auto-resolved). Pending items are never touched.

deepResearchPanel.noTasks:
No research tasks yet
Enter a topic above or click Deep Research in Review

cascadeDelete.warningsHint: These pages share source overlap with the page being deleted. The deletion will proceed, but review these after.
cascadeDelete.wikilinksCount: {{count}} dead [[wikilink]] will be neutralised to plain text
cascadeDelete.wikilinksCount_plural: {{count}} dead [[wikilinks]] will be neutralised to plain text
cascadeDelete.indexEntryRemoved: Entry in index.md will be removed
cascadeDelete.rawSourceDeleted: Raw source file will be deleted
cascadeDelete.noPagesDeleted: Only this page will be deleted.
cascadeDelete.noWikilinksToRewrite: No other pages reference this page.
cascadeDelete.noRawSource: No raw source file to delete.

noteView.selectPagePrompt: Select a page to read or edit
noteView.selectPageBody: Click any page in the tree on the left to open it here.
noteView.staleConflict: This note changed on disk — reload before editing
noteView.unsavedDialogBody: You have unsaved changes. Discard them and leave?

lint.title: Lint
lint.hint: Wiki health findings — fix or acknowledge each finding.
lint.runLintHelp: Run a bounded lint scan: structural checks + semantic review. Produces findings only — fixes are human-gated.
lint.empty: No open findings. Run a lint scan to check wiki health.
lint.emptyBody: Lint checks for orphan pages, missing cross-references, contradictions, stale claims, and missing pages. Click "Run Lint" to start.
lint.flagOnly: Flag-only — no file write on apply
lint.semanticHelp: When enabled, the scan includes an LLM semantic pass (contradiction, stale-claim). Disable for a free deterministic-only scan (broken links, orphans, missing xrefs).
lint.deleteConfirmHint: Click Delete again on the row to permanently remove this page

filter.spacingHelp: Adjusts visual spacing without changing graph layout (server coordinates preserved).

graph.regenerateTitle: Reconnect ingests to each other and recompute the graph layout
graph.regenerateNone: Graph up to date — no new links

insights.title: Graph Insights
insights.empty: No insights found. Add more pages and links to surface surprising connections and knowledge gaps.

community.lowCohesionWarning: Low cohesion — this community may be fragmented.
community.coldCache: Graph layout not ready — regenerate the graph first.

edge.notFound: No direct edge between these nodes.

graph.title: Knowledge Graph

search.title: Search
search.minCharsHint: Type at least 2 characters to search
search.emptyBody: Start typing to find pages by semantic similarity.
search.noResultsHint: Try different keywords or a broader query.

sources.title: Sources
sources.deletedFolderTooMany: Folder too large to delete — use the backend CLI.
sources.empty: Select a file to preview it
sources.emptyHint: No source files yet. Import a document to get started.
sources.noPreview: No preview available for this file type.

bulk.deleteDialogBody: Permanently delete {{count}} file(s) and their derived wiki pages?

providerGate.title: No provider configured
providerGate.body: Configure an AI provider in Settings before using chat or ingest.

activity.cancelAllConfirm: Cancel all active and pending tasks?
activity.cancelAllDialogBody: Cancel all running tasks? This cannot be undone.

connect.title: Synapse
connect.subtitle: Enter the address of your Synapse backend to get started.
connect.tokenPlaceholder: Leave blank if authentication is disabled
connect.tokenGateSubtitle: Authentication is required. Enter your access token to continue.

errors.scheme: Only http:// and https:// URLs are accepted.
errors.unreachable: Could not reach the server. Check the address and try again.
errors.status: Server returned an error ({{status}}). Check the address and try again.
errors.authRequired: Invalid or missing access token. Enter the SYNAPSE_AUTH_TOKEN configured on the server.

connect.detected: Synapse server detected on this machine

convert.title: Convert PDFs with Marker
convert.desc: Drop or select up to 10 PDF files. Synapse will convert them with the Marker microservice and auto-ingest the results into the wiki.
convert.markerOfflineTooltip: Marker offline — start the Marker microservice to enable conversion
convert.dropLabel: Drop PDFs here, or click to browse
convert.tooManyFiles: Too many files — select at most 10 PDFs at a time
convert.badFileType: Unsupported file type — only .pdf files are accepted
convert.successHint: Converted files will appear in the wiki after the watcher picks them up. Check the Sources panel to confirm.
convert.startMarkerError: Marker did not respond after 120 s — check the command and try again
convert.startMarkerConfigHint: Enter the shell command to launch the Marker microservice. Saved locally on this machine only.
convert.error409: A conversion is already running. Wait for it to complete.

launcher.title: Projects
launcher.subtitle: Open, create or switch between Synapse vaults. Each project is a vault folder.

previewPanel.bodyHint: Open the page in the Wiki to read its full content.

serverChip.label: Connected to {{host}} — click to switch server

update.error: Update failed. Retry on next launch.

config.error: Could not load settings. Is the backend running?

imageCaptioningSection.title: Image captioning
imageCaptioningSection.desc: Generate factual captions for images extracted from PDFs / DOCX / PPTX at ingest. Captions become alt text inside the source markdown, so semantic search can match image content. Cached by image hash so duplicate logos/charts only call the model once.

visionCaptionsEnabled.label: Caption images at ingest
visionCaptionsEnabled.help: Off: image references are stripped from ingested content — no captions, search won't match them. On: each new image gets a caption from the vision-capable provider (cached by hash) and lands in the page's alt text.

visionMaxImagesPerRun.label: Max images captioned per run
visionMaxImagesPerRun.help: Cap on how many images call the vision model per ingest run (cost/time bound). 0–50; 0 disables captioning even when the toggle is on.

pdfExtractor.label: PDF extractor
pdfExtractor.help: Choose which engine extracts text from PDF files on ingest.
pdfExtractor.optionMarker: Marker (higher quality, requires the Marker microservice)

markerServiceUrl.label: Marker service URL
markerServiceUrl.help: HTTP URL of the running Marker microservice. Only used when the PDF extractor is set to Marker.

markerTimeoutSeconds.label: Marker timeout (s)
markerTimeoutSeconds.help: Seconds to wait for the Marker microservice before timing out. Must be between 1 and 3600.

costAlertThresholdUsd.label: Monthly cost alert (USD)
costAlertThresholdUsd.help: Show an alert when monthly AI spend exceeds this amount. Set to 0 to disable the alert.

embeddingsEnabled.label: Vector embeddings
embeddingsEnabled.help: Enable semantic (vector) search against the wiki. When off, retrieval degrades to keyword-only mode.

embeddingFormat.label: Embedding format
embeddingFormat.help: Request/response format used to call the embedding model. Choose Ollama for local Ollama models, or OpenAI-compatible for other endpoints.

overviewLanguage.label: Overview language
overviewLanguage.help: Language used when Synapse generates overview pages. Leave blank for automatic detection from the vault content.

wikilinkEnrichEnabled.label: Auto wikilink enrichment
wikilinkEnrichEnabled.help: Automatically add [[wikilinks]] to related pages during ingest. Disable to speed up ingest at the cost of fewer connections.

domainVocabulary.label: Domain vocabulary
domainVocabulary.help: Comma-separated list of domain names. Pages ingested after this is set will be auto-tagged against this vocabulary (domain/Name tag convention). Empty = feature dormant, no provider calls.
domainVocabulary.chipHint: Enter domain names separated by commas. Do not include the domain/ prefix — it is added automatically.

pdfExtractorSection.title: PDF extraction
pdfExtractorSection.desc: Configure which engine extracts text from PDF files and how to reach the Marker microservice.

runtimeOverridesSection.title: Runtime overrides
runtimeOverridesSection.desc: These settings are persisted in the database and override the environment-variable defaults without restarting Docker.

limitsSection.title: Loop limits
limitsSection.desc: Cap the maximum iterations and token spend for bounded loops (I7). Changes take effect on the next run without restarting Docker.

deepResearchMaxIter.label: Deep Research — max iterations
deepResearchMaxIter.help: Maximum number of search-assess-refine cycles per Deep Research run. Prevents runaway loops (I7).

deepResearchTokenBudget.label: Deep Research — token budget
deepResearchTokenBudget.help: Maximum tokens consumed per Deep Research run across all iterations. Logged as total_cost_usd (I7).

deepResearchMaxQueries.label: Deep Research — max queries
deepResearchMaxQueries.help: Maximum SearXNG queries issued per Deep Research run.

lintMaxIter.label: Lint-fix — max iterations
lintMaxIter.help: Maximum number of lint-fix loop iterations before stopping and flagging for human review (I7).

lintTokenBudget.label: Lint-fix — token budget
lintTokenBudget.help: Maximum tokens consumed by the lint-fix loop per run. Logged as total_cost_usd (I7).

gettingStarted.wizardSlotDesc: A guided setup to connect your backend, choose an inference provider, and configure PDF extraction.

step1.title: Connect to backend
step1.desc: Verify that the Synapse backend is reachable. If you are running Synapse locally, it should already be connected.
step1.errNotOk: Backend returned status {{status}}. Check that the service is running.
step1.errUnknown: Could not reach the backend. Check the URL in Settings › Security.
step1.urlHelp: Leave blank to use the same origin as this page. Enter an http:// or https:// URL to point to a different backend.

step2.title: Choose an inference provider
step2.desc: Select the AI provider and model Synapse will use for ingest and chat. You can add more providers later in Settings › AI & Models.
step2.err: Could not save provider. Check the details and try again.

step3.title: Choose PDF extraction
step3.desc: Select how Synapse extracts text from PDF files. The built-in pypdf engine requires no extra services; Marker produces higher-quality output but needs the Marker microservice running.
step3.err: Could not save PDF extractor setting. Try again.

step4.title: All set!
step4.desc: Synapse is configured and ready. You can adjust these settings any time from Settings › Getting started.

home.title: System Overview
home.subtitle: At-a-glance view of your knowledge base

sections.title: SEZIONI
sections.emptyVocab: No domain vocabulary configured. Add domains in Settings › Advanced to see per-domain breakdowns.

activeJobs.title: LAVORI ATTIVI
activeJobs.backfillTagged: {{count}} pages tagged in the last run

placeholder.title: Server v1.1 — update to enable the Home dashboard
placeholder.body: The backend does not yet expose /stats/overview or /stats/sections. Update the backend to v1.2 to enable the Home dashboard.

versionBanner.message: Server is at v{{backendVersion}} — frontend expects v{{appVersion}}. Update the backend image to get the latest features.
